taxonomy: Lastrea squamulosa is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It is a type of epiphytic fern that is native to tropical Asia. The classification of this species includes the kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Athyriales, family Athyriaceae, genus Lastrea, and species L. squamulosa. This fern is also known as the scaly maiden-hair fern due to its fronds that resemble the hair of a maiden. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and the most recent taxonomic revisions.
